message: feat: expose r_ and c_ in fnp-python (franken_numpy-n011)

`isposinf`/`isneginf`/`signbit`/`isnan`/`isinf`/`isfinite`/`spacing`/`sign`/`floor`/`ceil`/`trunc`/`rint`/`degrees`/`radians`/`sinc`/`copysign`/`nextafter`/`hypot`/`ldexp`/`logaddexp`/`logaddexp2`/`frexp`/`modf`/`nan_to_num` oracle parity, `take(axis)` reshape/scalar semantics, in-place `put`/`place`/`putmask`/`fill_diagonal` copy-back semantics, `indices(dtype)` grid parity, `diag` vector/matrix offset parity, `diagflat` flatten/offset parity with precise dtype preservation, `diagonal(offset, axis1, axis2)` parity across arbitrary axes, `fill_diagonal(wrap)` rectangular and equal-length N-D parity with cyclic value assignment, `ix_` bool/nonzero normalization and empty-input dtype parity, dense/sparse `meshgrid(*xi, copy, indexing)` parity including empty, scalar, flattened, object, and writeback-view behavior, `mgrid[...]` dense slice-object parity including single-slice vs tuple shape, defaulted bounds, and float32/complex-count dtypes, `ogrid[...]` sparse slice-object parity including tuple return shape and dtype preservation, `ravel_multi_index(mode, order)` parity including empty and scalar cases, `unravel_index(order)` parity including 0-D rejection and shape-preserving integer outputs, diagonal/triangle index tuple parity, shape-only `*_from` wrappers for arbitrary ndarray dtypes, `axis=None` flatten semantics for `take_along_axis` and `put_along_axis`, overlap-safe view mutation parity, short-condition truncation parity, first-match `select` behavior, large-uint64 coverage, full `choose(mode='raise'|'wrap'|'clip')` parity, integer exponent tuple output for `frexp`, signed fractional/integral tuple parity for `modf`, scalar replacement parity for `nan_to_num`, and module export wiring |

 ## Remaining Gaps (Python-specific, low priority)
 
-1.  ## Intentional Design Decisions

@@ -660,6 +660,8 @@ FrankenNumPy implements the full set of NumPy's array construction and manipulat
 | `hstack` | Stack horizontally. For 1-D: axis 0. For N-D: axis 1. |
 | `dstack` | Stack along axis 2. Promotes to at least 3-D first. |
 | `column_stack` | Stack 1-D arrays as columns of a 2-D array. |
+| `r_[...]` | Row-wise concatenation object with slice expansion and NumPy directive fallback |
+| `c_[...]` | Column-wise concatenation object with 1-D-to-column promotion and NumPy directive fallback |
 | `block(grid)` | Assemble from nested grid. Concatenates within rows, then stacks. |
 | `split(ary, n, axis)` | Split into `n` equal sub-arrays along axis |
 | `array_split(ary, n, axis)` | Split allowing unequal sub-arrays |
